Article.ArticleTitle: Clinic Study on the Relationship Between BRAF V600E Mutation and Outcomes of Microwave Ablation in Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma: A Propensity Score-matched Cohort Study.

Abstract.AbstractText: Microwave ablation (MWA) has emerged as a minimally invasive alternative to surgery for selected cases of papillary thyroid carcinoma (PTC). Although the BRAF V600E mutation is a well-established predictor of aggressive clinicopathologic features in surgical cohorts, its influence on ablation outcomes remains unclear.

We retrospectively reviewed 683 patients with cytologically or histologically confirmed T1N0M0 PTC who underwent ultrasound-guided MWA between March 2016 and May 2024. Among them, 515 (75.4%) were BRAF-positive and 168 (24.6%) were BRAF-negative. Propensity score matching (PSM) was applied to balance demographic, clinical, and tumor characteristics. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), and the secondary endpoints were procedure-related complications and longitudinal changes in the volume reduction ratio (VRR).

After PSM (2:1), 336 BRAF-positive and 168 BRAF-negative patients were included. During a mean follow-up of 26 months, disease progression occurred in 1.49% (5/336) of BRAF-positive patients (4 new tumors and 1 nodal metastasis) and 1.19% (2/168) of BRAF-negative patients (1 new tumor and 1 nodal metastasis). The overall incidence of disease progression was low (1.49% vs. 1.19%; p > 0.999), with no significant difference in PFS between groups (log-rank p = 0.78; HR, 0.795; 95% CI, 0.154-4.101). The 1-, 3-, and 5-year PFS rates were comparable (5-year: 97.8% vs. 95.7%; all p > 0.999). The median time to complete ablation zone disappearance was 13 months in both groups (log-rank p = 0.51). The main complication was transient hoarseness; complication rates were low and comparable (4.76% vs. 4.17%, p = 0.825), and no procedure-related mortality occurred.
MWA achieved effective local control with low complication rates in T1N0M0 PTC regardless of BRAF V600E mutation status.
